question_text: Which sin is the GREATEST of all major sins and the only one Allah has declared unforgivable if died upon?

question_data.quranic_ref: إِنَّ اللَّهَ لَا يَغْفِرُ أَن يُشْرَكَ بِهِ
options: Shirk — Associating partners with Allah
Qatl — Murder
Zina — Adultery
Riba — Usury
question_data.explanation: Shirk is the greatest sin. Allah says He will not forgive it if one dies upon it (4:48), yet He forgives all lesser sins to whom He wills.

question_text: A Muslim who does NOT pray (abandons Salah deliberately) commits which level of sin according to Imam Dhahabi?

options: A minor sin requiring istighfar
A major sin (Kabira)
Kufr — disbelief (exit from Islam)
Makruh — disliked only
question_data.explanation: The Prophet ﷺ said: 'Between a man and shirk and kufr is the abandonment of prayer.' (Muslim). Many scholars classify deliberate abandonment as kufr.

question_text: What is the Islamic ruling on Sihr (magic/sorcery)?

options: Disliked but permitted if used for protection
A major sin and act of kufr
Only forbidden if it harms others
Allowed if done by religious scholars
question_data.explanation: Magic/sorcery is a major sin that can constitute kufr. Allah says the Shayateen taught people sorcery (2:102). It is listed among the seven destroyers (al-mubiqat).

question_text: The Prophet ﷺ listed seven destroyers (al-mubiqat). Which of the following is NOT one of them?

options: Shirk with Allah
Sorcery (Sihr)
Backbiting (Gheeba)
Devouring the wealth of orphans
question_data.explanation: The seven destroyers (Bukhari & Muslim) are: Shirk, Sorcery, Unjust killing, Riba, Devouring orphan's wealth, Fleeing from battle, Slandering chaste women. Gheeba is a sin but not in this list.

question_text: Consuming Riba (interest/usury) is a major sin. What does the Quran say awaits those who persist in it?

options: They will be forgiven if they give Sadaqah
They are declared at war with Allah and His Messenger
Their wealth will be reduced as punishment
They will only face punishment in the Hereafter
question_data.explanation: Quran 2:279: 'If you do not, then be warned of war from Allah and His Messenger.' This is the only sin in the Quran where Allah declares war on the perpetrator.

question_text: According to Imam Dhahabi, what makes something a Kabira (major sin) as opposed to a sagheera (minor sin)?

options: It is mentioned by name in the Quran
It carries a specific hadd punishment, curse, or warning of Hellfire in Quran/Sunnah
It is universally agreed upon by all scholars
It harms other people physically
question_data.explanation: Imam Dhahabi defines a kabira as any sin that carries a specific hadd punishment, or is explicitly cursed, or warned about with Hellfire/Wrath/Punishment in the Quran or authentic Hadith.

question_text: Disobeying and disrespecting one's parents (Uquq al-Walidayn) is a major sin. Which pairing from the Quran shows its severity?

question_data.quranic_ref: وَقَضَىٰ رَبُّكَ أَلَّا تَعْبُدُوا إِلَّا إِيَّاهُ وَبِالْوَالِدَيْنِ إِحْسَانًا
options: It is paired with the prohibition of killing
It is paired with the command to worship Allah alone (17:23)
It is paired with the prohibition of Zina
It is paired with the prohibition of theft
question_data.explanation: Quran 17:23 places worship of Allah alone immediately followed by excellence to parents, showing the gravity of disobedience to parents.

question_text: Cutting the ties of kinship (Qat' al-Rahim) is a major sin. The Prophet ﷺ said which person will NOT enter Jannah?

options: One who misses Friday prayer once
One who cuts ties of kinship (qati')
One who delays Zakat
One who does not perform voluntary fasting
question_data.explanation: The Prophet ﷺ said: 'The qati' (one who cuts kinship ties) will not enter Paradise.' (Bukhari & Muslim). Allah promised to join those who maintain it and cut off those who cut it.

question_text: Zina (adultery/fornication) is a major sin. What must a Muslim do if they fall into it?

options: Accept that they are permanently damned
Make sincere Tawbah — Allah forgives all sins for those who repent
Perform 100 days of fasting as expiation
Leave the country to escape the shame
question_data.explanation: Quran 25:68-70 lists Zina among the gravest sins, but immediately follows with: 'Except those who repent, believe, and do righteous deeds — for them Allah will replace their sins with good deeds.' Sincere Tawbah is always open.

question_text: Devouring the wealth of orphans is listed among the seven destroyers. What does the Quran say those who do this are consuming?

question_data.quranic_ref: إِنَّ الَّذِينَ يَأْكُلُونَ أَمْوَالَ الْيَتَامَىٰ ظُلْمًا إِنَّمَا يَأْكُلُونَ فِي بُطُونِهِمْ نَارًا
options: Stolen silver and gold
Fire in their bellies
The rights of the weak
The blessings of the community
question_data.explanation: Quran 4:10: 'Indeed those who devour the wealth of orphans unjustly are only consuming fire into their bellies, and they will be burned in a Blazing Fire.'

question_text: False testimony (Shahada al-Zur) and lying are major sins. The Prophet ﷺ repeated the warning about false testimony how many times?

options: Once
Twice
Three times — until companions wished he would stop
Seven times
question_data.explanation: In a famous hadith (Bukhari), the Prophet ﷺ warned about false testimony three times repeatedly, until the companions said 'we wished he would have been quiet.' This shows its enormous gravity.

question_text: Drinking alcohol (Khamr) is forbidden. The Prophet ﷺ cursed how many people connected to alcohol?

options: 3 people (drinker, seller, buyer)
7 people
10 people
Only the drinker
question_data.explanation: The Prophet ﷺ cursed 10 people connected to alcohol: the one who presses it, the one who has it pressed, the one who drinks it, the one who carries it, the one it is carried to, the one who serves it, the seller, the one who consumes its price, the buyer, and the one it is bought for. (Ibn Majah, Tirmidhi)

question_text: Qadhf (falsely accusing a chaste Muslim of Zina) is a major sin. What is the prescribed punishment (hadd)?

options: Imprisonment for one year
80 lashes and rejection of their testimony forever (unless they repent)
50 lashes and public apology
Financial compensation to the accused
question_data.explanation: Quran 24:4: 'Those who accuse chaste women and then do not produce four witnesses — lash them with eighty lashes and do not accept from them testimony ever after. Those are the defiantly disobedient.'

question_text: Fleeing from battle (Firrar min al-Zihaf) is one of the seven destroyers. In what context does this apply?

options: Fleeing from any difficult situation in life
Fleeing from an advancing enemy army in Jihad without valid reason
Fleeing from a natural disaster
Fleeing from one's responsibilities at work
question_data.explanation: This refers specifically to retreating from the battlefield without a valid excuse (like tactical retreat, or being overwhelmed). It is a betrayal of the Muslim army and community.

question_text: Suicide (killing oneself) is a major sin. What did the Prophet ﷺ say about its punishment?

options: The person is forgiven due to mental suffering
The person will repeatedly perform the same act of killing themselves in Hell
There is no punishment — it is only discouraged
The person exits Islam by this act
question_data.explanation: Bukhari: 'Whoever kills himself with an iron tool will be in Hellfire with that tool in his hand, stabbing himself repeatedly forever.' Allah alone gives and takes life.

question_text: Kibr (arrogance/pride) is a major sin. The Prophet ﷺ defined it as:

options: Thinking you are better than everyone at all times
Rejecting the truth and looking down on people
Being proud of one's achievements
Refusing to listen to advice
question_data.explanation: Muslim: 'Kibr is: rejecting truth and belittling people.' The Prophet ﷺ said 'No one with even a mustard seed of arrogance in his heart will enter Paradise.'

question_text: Which of the following describes the sin of Ghulul — stealing from war spoils?

options: Taking goods on credit and not repaying
Taking from the public treasury without permission
Taking anything from the enemy's spoils before proper distribution (Ghanima)
Hiding wealth to avoid Zakat
question_data.explanation: Quran 3:161: 'It is not for a Prophet to deceive (steal from spoils).' The Prophet ﷺ said the thief will carry what he stole on the Day of Judgment. Proper distribution of Ghanima is obligatory.

question_text: Not paying Zakat is a major sin. What punishment does the Quran mention for those who hoard gold and silver without paying Zakat?

options: Their wealth will be burned in this world
Their foreheads, sides, and backs will be branded with it on the Day of Judgment
They will be imprisoned in the Hereafter
Their children will inherit curses
question_data.explanation: Quran 9:34-35: 'Those who hoard gold and silver and do not spend them in the cause of Allah — give them tidings of painful punishment. The Day when it will be heated in Hellfire and seared upon their foreheads, sides, and backs.'

question_text: Thieft (Sariqah) is a major sin with a prescribed punishment. What is the hadd for theft in Islamic law?

options: 100 lashes and imprisonment
Cutting the hand of the thief (with conditions)
Paying back double what was stolen
Public shaming and community service
question_data.explanation: Quran 5:38: 'As for the thief, the male and female — cut their hands as recompense for what they committed as a deterrent punishment from Allah.' Strict conditions must be met before this hadd applies.

question_text: Highway robbery (Qat' al-Tariq) is among the most dangerous major sins. What range of punishments does the Quran prescribe?

options: Only monetary fine and imprisonment
Execution, crucifixion, cutting of alternate hands/feet, or exile
100 lashes and expulsion from the city
Death by stoning
question_data.explanation: Quran 5:33: 'The punishment of those who wage war against Allah and His Messenger and strive to make mischief: execution, crucifixion, cutting of hands and feet from opposite sides, or exile.' This is the most severe punishment range in the Quran.

question_text: A Yamin al-Ghamus is an oath that buries the swearer in sin/Hellfire. What makes this oath special?

options: An oath taken in the name of other than Allah
A deliberate false oath taken to seize another's right
An oath broken accidentally
An oath to do something impossible
question_data.explanation: Yamin al-Ghamus is swearing by Allah deliberately knowing you are lying, to gain something or harm someone. It is called 'ghamus' (immersing) because it drowns the swearer in sin and then in Hellfire.

question_text: Oppression (Dhulm/Zulm) is a major sin. What does Allah say about oppression in the Hadith Qudsi?

options: 'I permit it only against oppressors'
'I have made oppression forbidden upon Myself and made it forbidden between you — so do not oppress one another'
'Oppression in this life has no punishment in the Hereafter'
'Only the oppressed can seek revenge'
question_data.explanation: Hadith Qudsi (Muslim): 'O My servants! I have forbidden oppression for Myself and have made it forbidden amongst you, so do not oppress one another.' The oppressor's deeds will be transferred to his victim on Judgment Day.

question_text: Collecting taxes/tolls unjustly (Al-Maks) is a major sin. Why is the tax collector threatened with severe punishment?

options: Because taxes belong to the king, not the collector
Because they take people's wealth without right — a form of oppression and highway robbery
Because they often lie about the amounts
Because they prevent trade and commerce
question_data.explanation: Unjust tax collectors extort people's wealth without divine or legitimate authorization. The Prophet ﷺ warned: 'The collector of illegal taxes will not enter Paradise.' (Abu Dawud)

question_text: Consuming haram (forbidden) food and wealth — including from unlawful sources — affects one's Ibadah. What did the Prophet ﷺ say about one whose food is haram?

options: Their prayer still counts if they repent
His du'a is not answered — his food, drink, clothes, and nourishment are all haram
They only need to give extra Sadaqah
Their other good deeds compensate for this
question_data.explanation: Muslim: The Prophet ﷺ mentioned a man raising his hands to the sky saying 'O Lord! O Lord!' but his food is haram, his drink is haram, his clothing is haram, nourished with haram — 'so how can his du'a be answered?'

question_text: Murder — killing a soul unjustly (Qatl al-Nafs) — is one of the seven destroyers. What does the Quran say about killing ONE innocent soul?

question_data.quranic_ref: مَن قَتَلَ نَفْسًا بِغَيْرِ نَفْسٍ أَوْ فَسَادٍ فِي الْأَرْضِ فَكَأَنَّمَا قَتَلَ النَّاسَ جَمِيعًا
options: It is like killing a hundred people
It is like killing all of humanity
It requires blood money (diya) only
It is equal to committing Shirk
question_data.explanation: Quran 5:32: 'Whoever kills a soul — unless for a soul or for corruption in the land — it is as if he had slain all of mankind.' Conversely, saving one life is like saving all of humanity.

question_text: The sin of Nifaq (hypocrisy) places the Munafiqeen in the lowest level of Hellfire. What is the Arabic term for 'the lowest depths of Hellfire'?

question_data.quranic_ref: إِنَّ الْمُنَافِقِينَ فِي الدَّرْكِ الْأَسْفَلِ مِنَ النَّارِ
options: أَسْفَلُ سَافِلِينَ
الدَّرْكُ الْأَسْفَلُ
جَهَنَّمُ الْكُبْرَى
لَظَى الْعُظْمَى
question_data.explanation: Quran 4:145: 'The hypocrites will be in the lowest depths of the Fire (al-Darakil Asfal), and you will find for them no helper.' This is the worst level of Hellfire, reserved for those who outwardly claim Islam but inwardly deny it.

question_text: The signs of a Munafiq (hypocrite) are well-known. How many signs did the Prophet ﷺ mention in the famous hadith?

options: Two signs
Three signs
Four signs
Five signs
question_data.explanation: Bukhari & Muslim: 'The signs of the hypocrite are three: When he speaks, he lies; when he makes a promise, he breaks it; when he is trusted, he betrays.' A variant adds: when he argues, he is obscene.

question_text: Not performing Hajj when one is able to is a major sin. The Prophet ﷺ warned that such a person would die as:

options: A sinner requiring extra Sadaqah to compensate
A Jew or a Christian — dying not as a Muslim
A person with reduced reward
Someone whose Hajj is not accepted later
question_data.explanation: The Prophet ﷺ said: 'Whoever can afford to make the pilgrimage but does not, it matters not whether he dies as a Jew or a Christian.' (Tirmidhi). This shows the gravity of abandoning this pillar of Islam.

question_text: Qat' al-Rahim and uquq al-walidayn both relate to family. Which Quranic description warns that such people are cursed and cut off?

question_data.quranic_ref: وَيَقْطَعُونَ مَا أَمَرَ اللَّهُ بِهِ أَن يُوصَلَ
options: Those who break the covenant of Allah after ratifying it, sever what Allah has ordered to be joined, and spread corruption (2:27)
Those who deny the resurrection and the Judgment Day
Those who refuse to pay Sadaqah and help the poor
Those who argue against the Prophets
question_data.explanation: Quran 2:27 describes the losers as those who break Allah's covenant, sever what Allah ordered to be joined (kinship/family ties), and spread corruption. These are the characteristics of Hizb ush-Shaitan.

question_text: Imam Dhahabi's book 'Al-Kaba'ir' lists the major sins. The Arabic word الكبائر (al-Kaba'ir) means:

options: The forgotten obligations
The great/major sins
The acts of innovation (Bid'ah)
The prohibited foods
question_data.explanation: الكبائر is the plural of كبيرة (kabira), meaning 'great' or 'large.' It refers to major sins — those with specific punishments, curses, or warnings of Allah's wrath in the Quran or authentic Sunnah.
